blanket_summary.type_1: Blanket building and business personal property coverage across all insured locations
blanket_summary.type_2: Blanket business income and extra expense coverage

general_information.type_of_sport_1: Recreational co-ed indoor volleyball league for employees and regular customers
general_information.type_of_sport_2: 5K charity fun run team participating in local community races twice per year
general_information.extent_of_sponsorship: The business sponsors two informal athletic activities each year primarily focused on employee wellness and community engagement. For the indoor volleyball league, the company pays the team registration fees for one co-ed team of 10–12 adult players (a mix of employees and a few long-time customers), provides branded T-shirts, and allows limited use of the staff break room for post-game gatherings with non-alcoholic beverages and snacks. Participation is entirely voluntary, and players sign league waivers directly with the community recreation center; the business does not provide coaching or formal training, and there is no expectation that employees participate as a condition of employment. For the 5K charity fun run, the company covers entry fees for up to 20 adult participants per event, organizes group training runs on Saturday mornings on public sidewalks and trails, and promotes the event on its social media. All participants register individually with the race organizers and sign their waivers with the race, and we clearly communicate that the business is only sponsoring fees and not directing training intensity or performance. No minors are recruited or encouraged as part of these sponsored activities, there is no contact component to either sport, and we do not provide transportation or specialized equipment beyond T-shirts and water bottles.
general_information.other_business_1_type_of_business_or_loc: Administrative office for Willow & Pine Coffee Roasters, LLC (back-office operations, accounting, marketing, and online order fulfillment only)
general_information.other_business_1_operations: This location is a small second-floor professional office suite used exclusively for administrative and support functions of Willow & Pine Coffee Roasters, LLC. No food or beverage is prepared or served on-site, and there is no public walk-in traffic. Typical operations include bookkeeping, payroll processing, marketing and social media management, website maintenance, online customer service via email and phone, and coordination of wholesale orders to local cafes and grocery stores. A small area is used for packing and labeling shelf-stable roasted coffee bags for shipment using standard packing materials (cardboard boxes, paper filler, and adhesive labels). All shipping is conducted via USPS and UPS pickups; there is no loading dock or heavy equipment. There are 4–5 employees working standard weekday hours, and the office is not open evenings or weekends. No hazardous materials are stored, beyond normal office supplies (toner, cleaning sprays) and limited quantities of pre-packaged roasted coffee and branded merchandise such as mugs and T-shirts.
general_information.other_business_2_type_of_business_or_loc: Secondary retail coffee shop location operating under the same Willow & Pine Coffee Roasters, LLC entity
general_information.other_business_2_operations: This second location is a neighborhood retail coffee shop and café under the same ownership and branding as the primary insured location. Operations mirror a typical specialty coffeehouse: brewing and serving coffee and espresso beverages, tea, and non-alcoholic cold drinks; preparing light food items such as pastries, pre-made sandwiches, and salads supplied by licensed local bakeries and commissary kitchens; and selling whole-bean coffee, branded mugs, and merchandise. Hours are 6:30 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., seven days a week. There is seating for approximately 30 patrons, with a mix of tables, bar seating, and a small lounge area. No deep frying is conducted; food heating is limited to convection ovens and a small panini press. Staff includes baristas and a shift lead on duty at all times. The location occasionally hosts low-key community events like acoustic music nights and book club meetings, always ending by 8:00 p.m., with no alcohol service. Standard safety practices include regular cleaning, wet floor signage when mopping, employee training on handling hot liquids and equipment, and compliance with local health department regulations. No manufacturing or large-scale roasting occurs on-site; all roasting is done at the main roastery under separate coverage.
general_information.equipment_1_equipment: Commercial 2-group espresso machine occasionally loaned to local community events (e.g., charity fundraisers and school auctions) with our staff operating the machine on-site
general_information.equipment_2_equipment: Portable coffee urns and pour-over stations loaned to local nonprofits for morning meetings and community gatherings

remarks.remarks_line_1: Willow & Pine Coffee Roasters, LLC is a small, locally owned specialty coffee business headquartered in Tacoma, Washington, founded in 2018 by spouses Daniel Harper and Melissa Nguyen-Harper. The company operates a primary roastery and café, a secondary neighborhood café, and a small administrative office, all within a 15-mile radius. The business focuses on ethically sourced beans, sustainable packaging, and fostering a safe, community-oriented environment for customers and employees.
remarks.remarks_line_2: The primary insured location combines a mid-sized coffee roastery with a front-of-house café. All coffee roasting is conducted on a single 15-kg commercial roaster using natural gas and is vented according to local building and fire codes. The roastery area is separated from the customer seating area by a half wall and is not accessible to the public. Employees receive training on burn prevention, safe handling of hot liquids and equipment, emergency shutoff procedures for the roaster, and slip-and-fall prevention (including immediate cleanup of spills and placement of wet floor signs). Fire extinguishers are inspected annually, and emergency exits are clearly marked and kept unobstructed at all times. No alcohol is sold or consumed on the premises, and there is no drive-through service, which helps reduce traffic-related exposures in the parking area.
remarks.remarks_line_3: The company places significant emphasis on risk management and documentation. All third-party vendors who perform services on-site—such as espresso machine technicians, hood cleaning contractors, and janitorial services—are required to provide certificates of insurance naming Willow & Pine Coffee Roasters, LLC as an additional insured when appropriate. The business maintains an employee handbook that includes safety policies, incident reporting procedures, and guidelines for hosting small community events (e.g., live music or art shows), including maximum occupancy, noise limits, and clear rules prohibiting open flames or unauthorized electrical equipment. To date, the business has not experienced any major claims; minor incidents have been limited to a few superficial burns and one slip-and-fall without medical treatment, all of which were documented and led to small internal process improvements such as enhanced signage and additional non-slip mats behind the barista line.
